_comment: Verification OPTIONS + POLICY for the decomp workflow. This file does NOT auto-run anything. The reverser agent (the one you chat with) reads it and CHOOSES, per translation unit: (a) whether a fresh-eyes review pass is needed at all, and (b) which provider / model / thinking-level to use — then invokes that choice itself. Compile-gate settings are separate, in verify.config.json. See AGENTS.md > Verification.

providers._comment: Declare only the agentic systems you actually have (have:true). For each, list the models the reverser may pick and, per model, the allowed thinking range. 'invoke' tells the agent HOW to run that provider once it chooses it.

claude-code.command: claude -m {model} {thinking_flag} --dangerously-skip-permissions -p --append-system-prompt "Use sub-agents in the session when you can." "Read and review the packet at: {packet_path}"
claude-code._invoke_note: If the agent has tool capabilities to spawn sub-agents directly in-session (Agent/Task tools like invoke_subagent), it should choose to do so. Otherwise, or if invoke is changed to 'cli', the command template is run through the shell tool, substituting {model}/{thinking_flag}/{packet_path}. Verify command details on your install.

codex._invoke_note: When the reverser picks codex it runs this through its shell tool, substituting {model}/{thinking_flag}/{packet_path}. Point to the review packet file path rather than inlining body. Verify command details on your install.

antigravity._invoke_note: Antigravity CLI (agy), run via the shell tool, passing the packet path via -p or positional argument. {thinking_flag} has no default flag — add one here if your build exposes it. Verify command details on your install.

_thinking_note: Generic ordered levels are mapped dynamically to the provider's local thinking_ladder. Each model's 'thinking' is a boolean value indicating whether it supports reasoning effort. If a model has 'thinking': false, then {thinking_flag} in the provider command is substituted with an empty string. If 'thinking': true, {thinking_flag} is populated with the provider's thinking_flag template using the chosen level (e.g. --effort high). {packet_path} is substituted with the path to the review packet (e.g. progress/reviews/<tu>.md), never inlining the packet body directly to prevent escaping and command-injection bugs.

review._enabled_note: Master switch. false => never review; compile-gate + the reverser's self-check is the only gate and the reverser records the pass itself.
review._mode_note: tiered = the reverser classifies each compiled TU and chooses per selection_guidance. always = review every TU. never = same as enabled:false.

classify_hints.trivial: single self-contained idiom (CRC, rounding, accessor); no new types / struct-offset recovery; branchless or one simple loop; gate passed clean
classify_hints.standard: a few functions or one non-trivial function; modest control flow; at most a minor local type touch
classify_hints.complex: real type / struct-offset recovery; intricate or ambiguous control flow; pseudocode that needed interpretation; edits to shared headers that can ripple

selection_guidance._comment: Guidance for the reverser; it has final say per TU and may deviate with a noted reason.
selection_guidance.trivial: skip the review pass (gate-only); record pass with note 'trivial; gate-only'
selection_guidance.standard: review with the cheapest capable available model, low-to-medium thinking
selection_guidance.complex: review with a stronger available model, medium-to-high thinking
selection_guidance.escalate_when_unsure: if you doubt your own reconstruction (FP precision, signedness, guessed offsets, inferred data tables), bump model strength and/or thinking one notch regardless of class

automated_check._comment: Optional cheap deterministic pre-review gate (NO LLM): compare structural signals (call count, branch/loop count, return paths) between the original pseudocode and the reconstructed C++ within tolerance. A green result can let a standard TU skip the LLM review. NOT BUILT YET — enabling is a no-op until implemented.

batch._comment: One TU per pass unless you name a count in your prompt ('do 5'); then that many in a single pass, with one combined review pass over the batch.
